figures.caption: Table 1. Primary failure mode distribution for FP16 baseline on AppWorld test_normal (163 failed tasks). Categories are ranked by frequency. Confidence-weighted scores incorporate the evaluator’s classification confidence.
Table 7. Top failure modes by difficulty after full scaffolding (AWQ, 158 failed tasks).

Table 6. Failure mode comparison before and after scaffolding (AWQ, test_normal). Counts reflect primary failure classification across all difficulty levels. The scaffolded model has fewer total failures (158 vs. 163) due to improved task goal completion.

Table 3. Task goal completion (%) on AppWorld test_normal (168 tasks). FP16 = full precision with 12K context; AWQ = 4-bit quantized with 32K context. “Scaffold” denotes our three-tier inference scaffolding method. We report 95% Wilson confidence intervals for aggregate task goal completion rates.
Figure 2. Comparison of failure modes for tasks of difficulty 1 for baseline vs full scaffold for Qwen 3 8B AWQ, which is a quantized version of Qwen.

Table 4. Comparison with published AppWorld results (task goal completion, test_normal). Our results use Qwen3-8B on a single 24 GB GPU. For our models, and for DeepSeek-Coder 33B Instruct assuming the same 168-task evaluation set, we report 95% Wilson confidence intervals.
Figure 1. Our proposed modular architecture. Each module targets an important failure mode for small language models to increase performance.
